SIte specific Alternative Solution for the "Public Spa" in the building..OBC Clauses 3.12.2.1.(4)(a) and (b) require a public spa to be surrounded by a hard-surfaced pool deck that is required to have clear deck space of 900mm on all sides and 1800mm at the main entrance point. In consideration of the functional statements and objectives associated with OBC clauses 3.12.2.1.(4)(a) and (b), and in lieu of providing a hard surfaced pool deck on four sides, it is proposed to provide the following design characteristics/equipment to achieve sufficiency of compliance with the above noted OBC requirements with a pool deck on only two sides. 1. Two electrically insulated or non-conducting reaching poles, each 4.0 m in length (including the added length of the reaching loop)2. A deck space greater than 900mm in width (1800mm at the entrance on two sides at which decking is proposed (actually minimum deck widths are 3180mm and 2150mm)3. A water depth of less than 13500mm (actual depth is approximately 1200mm) and 4. A relatively small spa area (29.5) and shape providing high visibility and accessibility form the proposed two sides of decking to all portions of the spa.

This report has been prepared for 4800 Yonge Limited (Client) to develop an Alternative Solution to the OBC high- rise requirement to provide 2 h fire separated vestibules between one elevator shaft, which serves above-grade and below-grade levels, and the remainder of the floor area on Levels P3 through to Pl of the proposed building at 4800 Yonge Street in Toronto, Ontario. Specifically, it is proposed to provide 2 h fire separated vestibules to serve the elevator shaft on the above-grade levels, Levels 1 through 7, in lieu of providing 2 h fire separated vestibules on the below-grade levels, Level P3 through to Pl, as an alternative to complying with below-grade elevator protection. Further, it is proposed for the vestibules serving the elevator to have doors that swing inward (i.e. against the direction of exit travel). The elevator shaft below-grade opens into mechanical rooms on Levels P3 to Pl where spatial constraints prevent the construction of an elevator vestibule within these rooms.

The Ontario Line is a 15.6-kilometre stand-alone rapid transit ine that will connect the Ontario Science Centre to Exhibition/Ontario Place. Fifteen stations are proposed, with numerous connections to the broader transit network, including GO Transit rai services, the Toronto Transit Commission's subway Lines 1 and 2, the future Line 5 (Eglinton Crosstown LRT), as well as numerous bus and streetcar routes.The Ontario Line - Southern Civil, Stations and Tunnel (Project) is part of the Ontario line.Rapid transit stations are governed by OBC Division B, Section 3.13., that has specific provisions for stations.  Service rooms in the station are classified as being Group F, Division 3, Low Hazard Industrial Occupancy.The service rooms are required to comply with the egress reqirements under Division B, Part 3 of the OBC.  For the purpose of application of the egress requirements to back of house service rooms, the provisions of Article 3.3.1.5. and Article 3.4.2.5. are applied.The proposed design of the back of house service rooms at the station does not comply with the acceptable solutions in OBC.When the proposed design differs from the acceptable solutions of the OBC, compliance is permitted to be achieved by using alternative solutions that will reach the level of performance required by the applicable acceptable solutions, as permitted by OBC division A, Clause 1.2.1.1.(1)(b). OBC provides specific Objective and Functional statements relative to complying with the Sentence 3.13.5.10.(2) of the Code.An alternative solution approach to building code compliance is proposed relative to the 2012 Ontario Building Code for the egress requirements in the rapid transit station.

The demo scope includes full removal of the main building and garage with a 75% waste diversion rate as aligned with LEED v5 MRc5 standards.

Rev 01 - This is a permit revision application for the relocation of the High Voltage Switchgear Room & revised rerouting of the generator gas line. The original permit numbers are 22 219655 BLD 00 NB, 22 219655 HVA 00 MS and 22 219655 PLB 00. As requested, coordinated and approved by Toronto Hydro. The High Voltage Switchgear Room was relocated from the below grade parking level to the ground floor (Level 1). The associated drawings for this item which have been included in the permit revision are: A0.21 (Life Safety Plan), A0.31 (Door Schedule), A2.22b (Floor Plan Level 1 South), E202b (Electrical Floor Plan Level 1 South), E401 (Enlarged Electrical Room Plan #1), E702 (Panel Schedules), M302b (HVAC Floor Plan Level 1 South) and M701 (Mechanical Schedules #1). The rerouting of the generator gas line was relocated from the below grade parking level to the ground floor (Level 1) to address interferences with other services. The associated drawings for this item which have been included are: A2.21b (Floor Plan Level 1 South), A2.22b Floor Plan Level 1 South), A2.23b (Floor Plan Level 2 South), A2.24b (Floor Plan Level 3 South), M202b (Plumbing, Drainage, Fire Protection, Floor Plan Level 1 South), and M430 (Gas Riser Schematic).
